If Rocky shows signs of GI upset (vomiting, diarrhea, refusal to eat, excessive gas):
Record time and description in log.
Inform supervisor and follow facility health protocol.

Double-check Rocky’s identity using kennel card and collar.
If allowed, hide pills in a small amount of food or an approved pill pocket that is compatible with a sensitive stomach.
If pill is left behind, re-offer in a different small food bite and note in log.
Immediately record: date, time, medication name, dosage given, and your initials on the medication log.
Note any refusal, vomiting, or unusual reaction.
If a dose is missed or only partially taken, notify supervisor and follow facility protocol before making any changes.
Avoid rough, uneven, or slippery surfaces where possible.
Limit walk duration if Rocky shows fatigue or discomfort.
Focus: Potty break and gentle movement to loosen joints.

. Still, treat introductions cautiously due to age and arthritis.
Greet Rocky calmly: avoid sudden movements, loud voices, or rough handling.
Preferred contact: slow petting on shoulders, chest, and sides; avoid pressing on hips or lower back.
Limit handling sessions to what Rocky seems to enjoy; stop if he turns away, stiffens, or tries to leave.
If social contact is approved in his file:
Introduce only to known, calm, gentle dogs.
Use on-leash or barrier greetings first (sniff through fence, parallel walking).
End interaction immediately if Rocky shows stress or physical discomfort (stiffening, growling, hiding, limping).
Prioritize calm, one-on-one time (sitting with him on the floor, gentle petting) over high-energy activities.
: allow extra time to explore with nose rather than covering distance.

Running, jumping, fetch involving quick starts/stops.
Stairs, steep ramps, or high platforms.
Rough play or wrestling with other dogs or staff.
Morning or midday: simple food puzzle (using a portion of his meal if possible to avoid extra calories).
Afternoon: quiet one-on-one interaction (5–10 minutes of petting, brushing, or sniff game).
Evening: calm settling routine—petting, soft words while he relaxes in bed.
Approach from the side, speak softly, and let Rocky see and smell you before touching.
Support his body when helping him up; avoid pulling on legs or collar.
If Rocky struggles to stand, place a hand gently under his chest and hindquarters to help lift.
Use non-slip mats in areas where floors are smooth or slippery.
Whining, panting at rest, or licking joints.
Sudden change in gait, limping, or refusal to use a limb.
Note and report immediately per Section 11.
Check that collars/harnesses are fitted so Rocky cannot slip out.
Ensure gates, doors, and yard areas are securely latched before off-leash potty time (if allowed).
Never leave Rocky unsupervised in a new area or with other dogs.
Maintain visual contact during any dog-dog interactions.
Remove any hard, sharp, or cluttered objects Rocky could trip over.
Keep cleaning products and chemicals locked away; clean floors thoroughly and dry them before Rocky walks through.
Clean food and water bowls daily.
Disinfect kennel regularly following facility protocol, ensuring bedding is dry and replaced if soiled.
Appetite: eating full meals? Any vomiting?
Stool: normal consistency and frequency? Note any diarrhea or constipation.
Mobility: worsening stiffness, limping, refusal to walk?
Behavior: unusual lethargy, agitation, or disorientation?
Labored breathing, collapse, or inability to stand.
Sudden severe lameness or crying out when moving.
Notify supervisor or on-call manager immediately.
Prepare Rocky’s file and medication list to accompany him if transport to vet is required.
Log all incidents, signs, and actions taken with date, time, and staff initials.
Clean, soft bedding properly arranged for joint comfort.
Rocky is resting comfortably (no heavy panting, obvious distress, or pacing).
Kennel is secure and environment is quiet and dim for sleep.

13.1. At shift change, verbally update the next staff member on:
Timing of Rocky’s last meal and medication.
Any changes in mobility, appetite, or stool.
13.2. Ensure the log is complete and accurate so all staff can maintain consistent care for Rocky.
